title: astragalus moellendorffii
intro: Astragalus moellendorffii, also known as Mongolian milkvetch, is a perennial plant species native to China and Mongolia. It belongs to the legume family and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its adaptogenic properties and medicinal value. The roots and stems of the plant contain compounds like astragaloside IV, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing various health benefits such as improving immune function, reducing fatigue, and protecting against cardiovascular disease. Astragalus moellendorffii is often prepared as a decoction or capsule for herbal use, and it is also available in modern supplement forms, including extracts and tinctures.
